stages.title: Novice
stages.description: Basic grammar: articles, tenses, plurals, simple vocab.

stages.title: Intermediate
stages.description: Intermediate grammar: continuous tenses, comparative adjectives, tricky prepositions, and irregular plurals.

questions.question: She is much _____ than her brother.
We are going on vacation _____ July.
The meeting is scheduled _____ Monday morning.
He has been very good _____ math since childhood.
I have _____ waiting for the bus for an hour.
They _____ playing football when it started to rain.
He speaks French fluently, _____?
You must _____ your homework before dinner.
_____ many apples are left in the basket?
I don't have _____ money left in my wallet.
She has _____ to Paris twice this year.
He is the _____ boy in the entire school.
My new car is more _____ than my old one.
I usually prefer drinking tea _____ coffee.
He sincerely apologized _____ his careless mistake.
She has always been afraid _____ spiders.
They are looking forward _____ meeting you tomorrow.
The famous novel was written _____ Mark Twain.
I will call you as soon as I _____ at the station.
Unless you _____ hard, you will fail the exam.
If I _____ a bird, I would fly across the ocean.
Neither of the boys _____ here right now.
Each of the students _____ a unique textbook.
Please be quiet; she _____ television right now.
I usually _____ up at 7 AM on weekdays.
He accidentally cut _____ with a sharp knife.
We really enjoyed _____ at the birthday party.
This is the exact house _____ Jack built.
The man _____ car was stolen went to the police.
I have been living in this city _____ 2010.
She has worked at the company _____ five years.
Despite _____ tired, he kept working on the project.
You had _____ see a doctor about that cough.
Would you mind _____ the window, please?
